title: Boat Rental in Constanta: Costs, Tips and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Exploring the Romanian Black Sea coast from a boat is easy from Constanta, with prices starting around 150 EUR for a half-day motorboat. At the Tomis Marina or Portul Turistic you can browse boats for your dates on BoatHire24, which connects renters with local owners.

Where to set off in Constanta

Constanta offers several marinas and harbours for launching or boarding a rental boat. The main base is Tomis Marina, a modern port in the city centre with full facilities. Nearby, Portul Turistic (Tourist Port) offers additional berths and charter companies. Further north, the smaller harbour at Mamaia provides direct access to the beach resorts and the Mamaia Lagoon. For a more sheltered start, head to the Agigea Yacht Club on the southern side of the port entrance. You can browse boats for your dates in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (6-8m), ideal for coastal cruising and family trips, up to 6 guests, from 150 EUR half-day.
  2. Sailing yacht (9-12m), perfect for a day sail with wind assistance, up to 8 guests, from 300 EUR full day.
  3. Motor yacht, e.g. Azimut 58, luxury and speed for longer explorations, up to 10 guests, from 800 EUR full day. For details, see the Azimut 58 for hire on BoatHire24.
  4. Catamaran (12-15m), stable and spacious for groups, up to 12 guests, from 600 EUR full day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Constanta?

Prices depend on boat type, duration, and season, with typical half-day rates from 150 EUR and full-day from 300 EUR. One option worth a look is the Azimut 58 for hire.

  • Guided shared tour: from 50 EUR per person for 3 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): 150-250 EUR.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): 600-1,200 EUR.
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day (8 hours): 1,000-2,500 EUR.

All prices include fuel within the local area, but extras such as catering or water toys may apply. Booking through BoatHire24 gives clear price breakdowns per boat. You can also read how boat rental works to understand deposit and cancellation policies. You can also see more at check availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Constanta?

Romanian law requires a valid boating licence for private rentals without a skipper, but many boats are offered with a captain included. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

For bareboat charters, you need an ICC or equivalent national licence recognised in Romania. Most tour operators and motorboat rentals include a skipper, so no licence is needed for those. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking. High season runs from June to September, when the prevailing Etesian-like wind (the Crivăț) can create choppy conditions offshore.

Tip: In August, book a morning slot to avoid the afternoon breeze that builds up near Constanta.

At a glance

  • Price range: 150, 2,500 EUR per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 3 to 8 hours typical
  • Guest capacity: 6 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (commonly quoted)

Questions about boat rental in Constanta

Can I rent a boat for a week in Constanta?

Yes, multi-day charters are available, especially for catamarans and yachts, with discounts for weekly bookings.

What landmarks can I see from the water?

From the sea you can view the Constanta Casino, the Genoese Lighthouse, and the ruins of the ancient city walls.

Is it safe to sail to the nearby Greek islands?

Sailing to the Danube Delta or Bulgaria is possible with a capable boat and good weather, but open-water crossings require experience.

Constanta offers a convenient base to explore the Black Sea coast, with a range of boats from small motorboats to luxury yachts. To secure the best option, check availability on BoatHire24, where you can compare verified listings and book directly. If you own a boat in Constanta, consider listing it for rent and becoming a host.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Constanta and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
